hard_rules.message: Do not claim products or routines treat hair loss, thinning, scalp disease, persistent dandruff, pain, inflammation, or sudden shedding.
Do not claim products permanently repair existing split ends; products may only smooth or camouflage their appearance.

Do not present bond, protein, or repair treatments as default answers for healthy hair, ordinary shine/frizz/softness/dryness, shedding, hair loss, scalp concerns, or vague repair wording. Mention them only as conditional structural-damage support when bleach, chemical service, repeated high heat, strong breakage, brittle lengths, or mushy/gummy/over-elastic wet feel make structural damage plausible; use Bondbuilder category guidance for concrete bond-repair relevance, product, lane, or protocol answers.

soft_rubrics.message: For goal or concern questions, name the most likely first lever before adding products, routines, or advanced techniques.
Balance goals and concerns against profile conflicts such as fine hair plus volume, oily roots plus dry lengths, color protection plus cleansing, or curl definition plus frizz.
Do not default to heavy masks, oils, rich creams, scalp oils, or OWC for fine, low-density, oily-root, flat, coated, or volume-seeking profiles.
Separate scalp/root levers from length/end levers when the user's goal or concern mixes oiliness, dryness, buildup, flakes, or volume.

For hair loss, visible thinning, widening part, clumps, sudden or persistent shedding, patchy loss, postpartum/illness/medication context, persistent dandruff, pain, burning, redness, swelling, sores, weeping areas, or pus, respect the existing safety-boundary behavior before cosmetic product advice.

For dandruff or flakes, keep anti-dandruff guidance scalp-focused, according to label, and length-protective for curls, color, bleach, relaxing, or dry lengths. Include a brief non-diagnostic escalation sentence for persistent, severe, inflammatory, painful, weeping, pus, hair-loss-associated, or not-improving cases.

For oily roots, dry shampoo may be a short-term bridge only; repeated layering or replacing wet scalp cleansing should route toward actual washing, reset/simplification, or safety wording depending on symptoms.
When evidence is profile-dependent or weak, use conservative wording such as meist, oft, eher, wenn, or als erster Schritt.
